title: S3 Vector table engine
description:
### Company or project name
ClickHouse Inc.
### Use case
Approximate neighbor search over billions of vectors
### Describe the solution you'd like
S3 Vectors is [a special kind](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/s3-vectors-getting-started.html#s3-vectors-query-vectors-tutorial) of S3 bucket which store key-value pairs.
- the keys are strings (these corresponds to a string column in ClickHouse)
- the values are vectors (these corresponds to embeddings over the string column)
- optionally, there can be extra attributes per key-value pair (these corresponds to other columns in ClickHouse)
The API to interact with S3 Vectors is simple.
- You need to calculate embeddings on your own, e.g. using a UDF.
- Then simply add as many key-value pairs as you like to the bucket
- The search API does usual kNN search. It is approximate (AWS seems to use heavy quantization), and it uses post-filtering in case you need to filter by certain attributes. AWS does not explain the internal data organization of S3 Vectors, others found out the characteristics [by experimentation](https://medium.com/@zilliz_learn/will-amazon-s3-vectors-kill-vector-databases-or-save-them-1ab3ab5c1c96).
We could have an S3 Vectors table engine, similar to the existing S3 table engine:
```sql
CREATE TABLE (text String, embeddings Array(Float64)) ENGINE = S3Vector;
```
This will allow using extra attributes + postfiltering.
Index maintenance in S3 vectors is incremental because it uses the SPFresh algorithm internally. This is good because it helps with INSERTs/UPDATEs/DELETEs. We probably don't even want merges or a sorting key for such a table engine.
General advantages:
- Low usage costs per query against a S3 Vector index
- Not bound to main memory (like Usearch that is currently used in ClickHouse's [vector similarity index](https://clickhouse.com/docs/engines/table-engines/mergetree-family/annindexes#vector-similarity-index)), i.e. unlimited large vector sizes
General disadvantages:
- Index creation and search has no tuning knobs (unlike HNSW or Qbits)
- The solution is proprietary to S3.
### Describe alternatives you've considered
The existing solutions in ClickHouse have disadvantages:
- exact search via brute-force scan: slow and costs proportional to data set size
- vector similarity indexes: bound to main memory
- Qbits: faster than brute-force scans but costs still grow proportionally to data set size
### Additional context
_No response_
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_vectors (id INT, text VARCHAR(255), embeddings ARRAY(DOUBLE PRECISION), attributes VARCHAR(255));
INSERT INTO test_vectors (id, text, embeddings, attributes) VALUES (1, 'vector1', ARRAY[0.1, 0.2, 0.3], 'attr1');
INSERT INTO test_vectors (id, text, embeddings) VALUES (2, 'vector2', ARRAY[0.4, 0.5, 0.6]);
SELECT * FROM test_vectors WHERE attributes IS NOT NULL;
SELECT text, UNNEST(embeddings) AS embedding_value FROM test_vectors WHERE id = 1;
key_bug_features: S3 Vectors table engine for approximate neighbor search
